data.title: An address deposited 3,000 ETH into an exchange at a market peak; if sold, it would have made a profit of $990,000.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, address 0xC45...D42DD withdrew 3000 ETH from Binance on February 24th at an average price of $1852.67, and then deposited it back to the exchange 7 hours ago when ETH rose to a recent short-term high, at a deposit price of $2183. If all of it were sold, this address would have made a profit of approximately $990,000.

data.title: Musk Hints at X Money's Internal Testing; Eric Trump Criticizes U.S. Banks
data.description:
On March 5, Elon Musk shared a post suggesting that leaked images of X Money's internal testing are genuine. According to BlockBeats, the images indicate that X Money offers an annual deposit yield of up to 6% and includes a debit card feature with cashback benefits.

Meanwhile, Eric Trump, son of U.S. President Donald Trump, criticized major U.S. banks such as JPMorgan and Wells Fargo this morning. Eric Trump claimed that the banking industry's political lobbying aims to prevent Americans from accessing higher yields and attempts to undermine stablecoins to maintain their monopoly. Recently, a new debate has emerged over stablecoin yields amid the legislative process of the Cryptocurrency Market Structure Act, also known as the CLARITY Act.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Experiences Significant Liquidations
data.description:
In the past 24 hours, the cryptocurrency market has seen liquidations totaling $594 million, according to ChainCatcher. Data from Coinglass indicates that long positions accounted for $116 million, while short positions amounted to $479 million in liquidations.

Bitcoin long positions experienced liquidations of $40.13 million, whereas Bitcoin short positions saw $275 million in liquidations. For Ethereum, long positions faced liquidations of $25.34 million, and short positions were liquidated for $131 million.

data.title: China's 2026 Debt Issuance Target Aligns with Market Expectations
data.description: China's debt issuance target for 2026 is aligning with market expectations, providing relief to bond traders. Bloomberg posted on X that this alignment is supported by ample liquidity and increasing speculation on policy easing. The move is seen as a positive signal for the bond market, which has been closely monitoring China's fiscal strategies. Traders are optimistic that the government's approach will maintain stability and support economic growth. The alignment with market expectations is expected to bolster confidence among investors, who have been concerned about potential disruptions in the bond market. This development comes amid a backdrop of global economic uncertainties, where China's fiscal policies play a crucial role in influencing market dynamics.

data.title: U.S. Military Reaffirms Commitment to South Korea Amid Middle East Considerations
data.description: The United States military has reiterated its commitment to defending South Korea against threats from North Korea, which possesses nuclear capabilities. Bloomberg posted on X, following reports from local media suggesting that Washington might be contemplating the redeployment of regional U.S. military assets to the Middle East. Despite these considerations, the U.S. military emphasized its ongoing dedication to ensuring the security of South Korea in the face of potential threats from the North.

data.title: India's Power Trading Volume Sees Significant Growth in February
data.description: India's energy trading sector experienced a notable increase in February, with power trading volumes rising by 30.4% compared to the same period last year. According to Jin10, this surge reflects a growing demand for electricity across the country, driven by industrial expansion and increased consumption. The Indian Energy Exchange (IEX) reported that this growth is indicative of the country's robust economic activities and the ongoing efforts to enhance energy infrastructure. The rise in trading volumes also highlights the importance of efficient energy distribution systems to meet the rising demand. As India continues to develop its energy sector, the focus remains on sustainable and reliable power solutions to support its economic growth.

data.title: Goldman Sachs CEO Predicts AI to Boost Productivity
data.description: Goldman Sachs Chair and CEO David Solomon expressed optimism about the impact of artificial intelligence on productivity. Bloomberg posted on X that during an interview with Haidi Stroud-Watts on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade," Solomon highlighted AI's potential to significantly enhance efficiency across various sectors. He emphasized that the integration of AI technologies could lead to substantial improvements in productivity, benefiting businesses and economies globally. Solomon's remarks underscore the growing importance of AI in shaping future economic landscapes.

data.title: Queenbee Coin Officials Accused of Stealing Seized Bitcoins
data.description: Officials from Queenbee Coin are facing accusations of orchestrating the theft of around 22 BTC, which were seized by the Seoul Gangnam Police in May 2022. According to NS3.AI, the theft involved the use of a known mnemonic code to transfer the stolen Bitcoins, valued at approximately $1.5 million. Arrests were made following witness testimonies and circumstantial evidence that confirmed meetings among Queenbee employees on the day of the theft.

data.title: Anthropic Resumes AI Safety Talks with U.S. Defense Department
data.description: Anthropic's CEO, Dario Amodei, has re-engaged in discussions with the U.S. defense department concerning AI safety measures. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that these talks had previously broken down last week. The Financial Times, citing several informed sources, reported on the renewed dialogue aimed at establishing safety guardrails for artificial intelligence.

data.title: Bitwise donated $233,000 to Bitcoin open-source developers, marking its second annual donation.
data.description: Bitwise announced on its X platform that it is donating $233,000 to Bitcoin open-source developers as part of its annual commitment to support them. This donation follows significant growth in the Bitwise Bitcoin ETF (BITB) this year and is the second annual contribution to BITB's launch commitment to donate 10% of its gross profits each year. The funds will be distributed among three non-profit organizations: Brink, OpenSats, and the HRF Bitcoin Development Fund.

data.title: US media: US officials facilitated a 1,000-kilogram gold deal between the US and Venezuela.
data.description: Two sources revealed that Venezuela's state-owned mining company signed a multi-hundred-million-dollar agreement on Monday to sell up to 1,000 kilograms of gold to the U.S. market, demonstrating increasingly close commercial ties between Venezuela and the United States. One source stated that the gold deal requires Venezuelan state-owned enterprise Minerven to supply 650 to 1,000 kilograms of gold ingots to commodities trader Trafigura. The contract stipulates a final gold content of 98%. Currently, one kilogram of pure gold costs approximately $166,000. Another source indicated that Trafigura will ship the gold to U.S. refineries under a separate arrangement with the U.S. government. U.S. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum arrived in Venezuela on Wednesday to discuss oil and mineral opportunities and helped facilitate this gold contract. (Jinshi)

data.title: Soulcast co-founder's address was poisoned, resulting in a loss of approximately $24 million (aEthUSDC).
data.description: According to PeckShieldAlert, an address (0xd2e8...ca41) associated with Soulcast co-founder @sillytuna had approximately $24 million worth of aEthUSDC stolen in an address poisoning attack. Currently, approximately $20 million worth of DAI is held in two attacker-controlled staging wallets, 0xdCA9...c9C4 (approximately $10 million) and 0xd0c2...dd3e (approximately $10 million), and has not yet undergone mixing. The attackers have begun bridging small amounts of funds to Arbitrum.

data.title: Elon Musk's X Money App Begins Limited Beta Testing with Cashback and Yield Features
data.description:
Elon Musk's new payment application, X Money, has initiated limited external beta testing this week, showcasing features such as cashback and yield on deposits. According to Cointelegraph, Hollywood actor William Shatner, known for his role as Captain Kirk in the original Star Trek series, is among the beta testers invited to participate. Screenshots shared by Shatner reveal that X Money users can earn cashback on specific card purchases and receive a 6% annual percentage yield on deposits.

Deposits within X Money are held by Cross River Bank, a member of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, ensuring insurance coverage up to $250,000 per person. X Money is part of Musk's ambitious vision to transform X into an 'everything app,' encompassing payments, private messaging, AI chatbot services through Grok, creator content, identity, and more. Musk described the app as a 'game changer,' aiming to centralize all monetary transactions.

Shatner has utilized the $42 sent to him by Musk to raise funds for charity, auctioning 42 X Money beta invites for $1,000 each with X's permission. Each winning bidder receives a $25 welcome gift card from X and $1 initially sent to Shatner by Musk. Following the first auction, Shatner and X conducted a second round, auctioning another 166 beta invites at the same price. Eligibility requires users to be U.S. residents over 18 with an active X account in good standing.

Despite Musk's known appreciation for Dogecoin (DOGE), there is no indication of crypto integration into X Money. The app marks Musk's return to the payment space, having founded X.com in the late 1990s before its merger into PayPal. In recent years, X has obtained money transmitter licenses in over 40 U.S. states and registered with the Financial Crimes Enforcement Network to facilitate peer-to-peer payments on the platform.

data.title: Tech Companies Urge Trump to Reassess Anthropic's National Security Risk Designation
data.description: Tech industry groups, representing major companies such as Google and Apple, are calling on U.S. President Donald Trump to reevaluate the decision to label Anthropic as a national security threat. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the concerns raised by these groups about the potential implications of this designation on the tech sector. The industry representatives argue that the move could have significant repercussions for innovation and collaboration within the technology field. They emphasize the importance of maintaining a balanced approach to national security that does not hinder technological progress. The appeal to the President underscores the ongoing dialogue between the tech industry and the government regarding security and innovation.

data.title: Understanding Market Signals: A Rational Approach to Crypto Predictions
data.description:
Crypto KOL Murphy posted on X, expressing appreciation for the positive feedback on the effectiveness of institutional signal BRS. He noted that while the immediate impact of signals, such as 'morning post, afternoon rise,' is gratifying, it is often a matter of luck and coincidence. Murphy emphasized that no single indicator or signal can predict market trends with 100% accuracy; rather, they offer probabilities.

Murphy encouraged followers to focus on the data changes and underlying logic that influence market movements in the short, medium, and long term. He has been analyzing various aspects such as changes in long-term holder behavior, gaps in chip structure, real cost pressures, and extreme market sentiment. These analyses are not based on luck but on objective and rational assessments.

Murphy cautioned against focusing solely on predictions of price movements and the correctness of opinions, as this can lead to fluctuating perceptions of his expertise. He stressed that no one can foresee the future, but experience, knowledge, and methodology can provide insights. Murphy believes that these insights are accessible to everyone willing to learn and understand the market dynamics.

data.title: Valence Introduces Trading Superapp for Prediction Markets
data.description: Y Combinator posted on X about Valence, a new trading superapp designed for prediction markets. The platform allows users to trade and execute strategies across multiple exchanges through a single interface. Valence offers a unified orderbook and API, facilitating streamlined trading operations. The app has already seen over 1 billion contracts traded, highlighting its growing popularity and utility in the trading community.

data.title: German robotics startup Neura has raised approximately €1 billion in funding, with Tether participating.
data.description: German robotics startup Neura Robotics is raising approximately €1 billion (about $1.2 billion) in funding, with stablecoin issuer Tether Holdings SA participating. The funds will be used to develop AI-driven humanoid robots. This round values ​​the company at approximately €4 billion, with further funding potentially possible. Headquartered in Metzingen, Germany, Neura Robotics currently sells factory transport robots and robotic arms for home use. CEO David Reger previously stated that the company received nearly $1 billion in orders last year, with clients including Japanese companies Kawasaki Heavy Industries and Omron. In January 2025, the company completed a €120 million funding round led by Exor's Lingotto Investment Management. This investment is the latest move by Tether to expand beyond its crypto business; Tether has previously invested in brain-computer interface company Blackrock Neurotech and Italian robotics startup Generative Bionics.

data.title: CZ: Buying up UAE real estate at rock-bottom prices?
data.description: CZ posted on the X platform that he had discovered an interesting website and asked, "Should I buy (UAE property at rock-bottom prices)?", while clarifying that this was not financial advice. The post referenced a tweet from Panic Selling, which announced that its platform now tracks the luxury rental market in Dubai and Abu Dhabi, stating that it will notify users immediately if landlords lower prices. Panic Selling reportedly scans over 20,000 UAE luxury property listings daily and plans to expand to more cities. CZ has followed the account.

data.title: US stocks closed with a strong rebound in the crypto sector, with Coinbase rising over 14%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the close of trading on the US stock market, the Nasdaq rose 1.29%, the S&P 500 rose 0.78%, and the Dow Jones rose 0.49%. The crypto sector collectively strengthened, with Solana rising 16.67%, Coinbase rising 14.57%, SharpLink rising 11.98%, Strategy (MSTR) rising 10.37%, Bitmine rising 7.92%, and Circle rising 5.66%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: A deputy to the National People's Congress stated that China's independently developed blockchain underlying technology has been applied in 16 central ministries and 27 central enterprises.
data.description: Dong Jin, a deputy to the National People's Congress and president of the Beijing Microchip Blockchain and Edge Computing Research Institute, stated that my country's independently developed blockchain underlying technology has been applied to 16 central ministries and 27 central enterprises, achieving positive progress in areas such as taxation, cross-border trade, and global payments. Tens of billions of invoices are processed annually on my country's domestically developed blockchain, with over 300,000 enterprises involved in cross-border trade on the blockchain, representing a trade volume of trillions of yuan. (CCTV News)
